name: server-actions
context: Mutate data in Next.js with Server Actions — 'use server' functions for forms, revalidation, redirects, and optimistic updates
info: Server Actions are asynchronous functions that run on the server, invoked directly from React components. They are defined with the `'use server'` directive and are the primary way to handle form submissions and data mutations in the App Router. Server Actions can be called from both Server and Client Components, support progressive enhancement (forms work without JavaScript), integrate with React's `useActionState` for pending states and validation errors, and can trigger revalidation of cached data via `revalidatePath()` and `revalidateTag()`. They replace the need for separate API routes for most mutation use cases. Server Actions are the idiomatic way to handle mutations in Next.js App Router applications.
triggers: create a form with server action
add form validation with server action

sections.title: Defining Server Actions
sections.body: Mark a file with `'use server'` at the top to make all exports Server Actions. Or add `'use server'` inside an async function in a Server Component for inline actions. Actions in a separate file can be imported by both Server and Client Components. Inline actions can only be used in the Server Component where they are defined.

sections.title: Form integration
sections.body: Pass Server Actions to the `action` prop of `<form>`. The action receives `FormData` as its argument. Forms with Server Actions support progressive enhancement — they work without JavaScript. Use `useActionState` to get pending state and return values (validation errors). Use `useFormStatus` in child components to detect pending state for submit buttons.

sections.title: Revalidation after mutations
sections.body: Call `revalidatePath('/path')` to invalidate the cache for a specific route, or `revalidateTag('tag')` to invalidate all fetches tagged with that string. Both functions are imported from `next/cache`. Use `redirect('/path')` from `next/navigation` after mutations to navigate the user to a new page. Revalidation and redirect must be called after the database mutation, not before.

sections.title: Optimistic updates
sections.body: Use React's `useOptimistic` hook to immediately update the UI before the Server Action completes. Pass the current state and a reducer function. The optimistic state shows instantly while the action runs. If the action fails, React reverts to the previous state. Combine with `useTransition` for non-form Server Action calls from event handlers.

sections.title: Security considerations
sections.body: Server Actions create public HTTP POST endpoints. Next.js provides CSRF protection by checking Origin/Host headers and encrypting action IDs. Always validate inputs — never trust FormData blindly. Use `zod` or similar for schema validation. Check authentication and authorization inside every Server Action. Never pass sensitive data through hidden form fields. The `server-only` package prevents server code from leaking to the client.
